subtitle: A 500-meter pine-and-dune cove near the resort town of Cala Rajada, kept free of hotels because the 1991 Balearic natural-areas law made it a protected ANEI.
spike_statement: One of the last undeveloped cove-and-dune systems on Mallorca's built-up northeast coast, protected from hotels by the 1991 Balearic natural-areas law.

levers.photo_tone: Pale quartz sand and marram-grass dunes under a dark ridge of Aleppo pines, turquoise Mediterranean water off an east-facing cove, the built resort town of Cala Rajada just out of frame to the south
